source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Five students carry out a run. Their results are recorded in the graph opposite, according to the time taken (Zeit) and the distance covered (Strecke). Who had the greatest average speed?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to a 'graph opposite' for the students' results
the needed quantities (time and distance) are stated as being recorded in the graph, not in the text
multi_step: solving requires reading both time (Zeit) and distance (Strecke) for each student from the graph
the target quantity is average speed, which requires combining distance and time rather than reading a single value
the task then requires comparing the computed/read inferred speeds across five students
verifiability: answer is a short_text person name and also matches one of the provided multiple-choice options
there is a single stated gold answer: 'Doris'
the result can be checked by comparing the speed implied by each student's graph point/record

risk_flags: exact graph values are not available in the text, so verification depends on successful image reading

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantics are intact
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scores indicate strong image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and high verifiability
although verification depends on reading the graph, the required graph image is available
